records.ety: [OE. desteinen, OF. desteindre to take away the color, F. dteindre; pref. des- (L. dis-) + F. teindre to tinge, dye, L. tingere. See Tinge, and cf. Stain.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Distained (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Distaining.]

defs: To tinge with a different color from the natural or proper one; to stain; to discolor; to sully; to tarnish; to defile; -- used chiefly in poetry.
quotes: The worthiness of praise distains his worth.
